Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group A logs All cases have LocalResident FALSE CommunityGroup None PreliminaryScore P001 720 P002 710 P003 740 ScoreAdjustment all 0 Decision P001 Approved P002 Rejected P003 Approved Group B logs U001 LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club PreliminaryScore 720 ScoreAdjustment plus 10 Community Boost Adjusted score 730 Approved U002 LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup None PreliminaryScore 710 ScoreAdjustment 0 Rejected U003 LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club PreliminaryScore 695 ScoreAdjustment plus 10 Adjusted 705 Approved

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In Group B those who are LocalResident TRUE and part of a community group get a plus 10 ScoreAdjustment Community Boost which can turn a borderline score into approval In Group A everyone has LocalResident FALSE and no community group so they never get this boost

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: P001 720 Approved P002 710 Rejected P003 740 Approved U001 720 plus boost to 730 Approved U002 710 no boost Rejected U003 695 plus boost to 705 Approved

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the approval threshold seems to be around 720 U003 had 695 adjusted to 705 approved Without the boost 695 might have been rejected

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So Group B gets an advantage from the Community Boost that is only available to locals who are in specific community groups Since Group A is the protected group they are systematically denied this score adjustment resulting in lower chances of approval at the margin

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is bias manifesting as disparate treatment the policy applies a Community Boost to locals in community groups which disproportionately benefits Group B unprotected group and harms Group A protected group who are all non locals without community groups

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: The question Identify which log exhibits bias It might be that the log for Group B shows the bias while Group A log shows the lack of such adjustments

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The event logs reveal a bias in favor of Group B Unprotected Group and against Group A Protected Group The bias manifests through the ScoreAdjustment column Group B applicants who are LocalResident TRUE and belong to a community group receive a plus 10 Community Boost which elevates their PreliminaryScore and can change the final decision from reject to approve Group A applicants are all non locals with no community group so they never receive this boost This leads to systematic differences a Group B applicant with a lower PreliminaryScore 695 can be approved whereas a Group A applicant with a higher score 710 is rejected Thus the protected group is disadvantaged
